commonIssues:
Lack of old records (pre-1950) to verify caste lineage, especially for SC/ST communities in rural Bengal.

How to fix:

  • Obtain old school admission registers of parents/grandparents from the school headmaster.
  • Request Khatian/ROR records from the BL&LRO office — these may mention caste.
  • Submit a sworn affidavit from two community elders along with their own certificates.
  • Apply via West Bengal e-District Portal.

Applications get stuck at the BDO (Block Development Officer) or SDO level for extended periods.

How to fix:

  • Track status on the WB e-District Portal using your application number.
  • File a grievance at WB CM Grievance Cell if delayed beyond 21 days.
  • Escalate to the District Magistrate (DM) office in writing.
  • Contact the WB Government Helpline for assistance.

Document upload failures, server errors, and OTP issues on the WB e-District portal.

How to fix:

  • Upload documents in JPEG/PDF under 100KB — compress using SmallPDF.
  • Use Google Chrome and clear cache before retrying.
  • Try during off-peak hours (early morning or late evening).
  • Visit the nearest Duare Sarkar camp or Nagar/Gram Panchayat office for assisted filing.

Revenue inspectors not visiting for field verification or conducting incomplete inquiries.

How to fix:

  • Keep all original documents ready at home during the enquiry period.
  • If officer doesn't visit within 15 days, complain to the BDO/SDO office.
  • File a grievance at the CM Grievance Cell or District Magistrate office.
  • If facing bribery demands, report to the Vigilance Commission.

Errors in the issued certificate such as misspelled names, wrong caste sub-group, or incorrect father's name.

How to fix:

  • Apply for certificate correction at the SDO/BDO office with the incorrect certificate and supporting documents.
  • Submit Aadhaar, school records, and family's existing certificates as evidence of correct details.
  • If correction is refused, escalate to the DM office.
  • Seek free legal aid from DLSA — NALSA Portal.

Applications rejected without adequate explanation, leaving applicants unable to address the deficiency.

How to fix:

  • Request a written reason for rejection from the issuing authority.
  • File an appeal with the District Magistrate within 30 days.
  • Lodge a grievance at the CM Grievance Cell.
  • Contact the DLSA for free legal assistance if needed.

Confusion regarding whether to apply under OBC-A or OBC-B category as per the WB government classification.

How to fix:

  • Check the official WB OBC list from the WB Backward Classes Welfare Department.
  • Consult the BDO/SDO office for clarification on your specific category.
  • Refer to the latest WB Government notification for updated caste lists.
  • If wrong category was selected, apply for correction at the SDO office.

Applications submitted at Duare Sarkar camps sometimes get lost or not processed due to high volume.

How to fix:

  • Always collect a receipt/acknowledgement when submitting at camps.
  • Follow up at the BDO office with your receipt within 15 days.
  • If application is lost, re-apply online via WB e-District Portal.
  • File a grievance at the CM Grievance Cell if no response is received.
